notes: Large-scale empirical study on a curated 5,000-file Python dataset from CodeContests: runs six LLMs to refactor code, uses passing unit tests as an oracle, and compares break rates by CodeHealth group with statistical tests (Mann-Whitney, chi-square, Holm correction) plus decision-tree/logistic-regression predictive modeling. It is a controlled comparison over a constructed benchmark dataset, not repository-mining or a human-subjects study; no human participants are involved.
